text:
Once upon a time in a small village nestled among mountains, there lived a curious owl. This owl was always up during the day basking in the sun, peering through the forest canopy fascinated by the wonders hummingbirds brought to the flowers. Despite never seeing any animals besides insects, it spoke every night a wonderful language of song.

One evening, its keen eyes spotted something unusual—a tiny bird fluttering among the moonlit leaves. Excited, it swooped down, catching the bird mid-flight. To its astonishment, it was a baby squirrel with big brown eyes full of wonder. Rather than releasing it, it decided to keep the little critter as a pet, naming it Sparkle.

Sparkle became known across the woods for its cheerful songs at dawn and dusk. The owl didn't know squirrels could sing, but it enjoyed its company immensely. Even more fascinating to the owl was that Sparkle had learned to mimic everything it heard—the birds singing, the stream trickling,
